name: Dubois Pike Warrick Economic Opportunity Committee, Inc. D/B/A Tri-Cap
description_short: Non-profit providing free housing, health, education, and financial assistance to low-income families across 38 Indiana counties since 1966.
description_long:
Dubois Pike Warrick Economic Opportunity Committee, Inc., operating as TRI-CAP, is an independent non-profit organization established in 1966 that serves low-income individuals and families across southern and central Indiana. With a presence in 38 counties including Dubois, Pike, Warrick, Vanderburgh, and many others, TRI-CAP has built a 57+ year track record of community support focused on self-sufficiency and family empowerment.

TRI-CAP offers a comprehensive suite of free services spanning three core areas: housing assistance (utility bill payment, rent/mortgage assistance, energy conservation, home repairs, down payment assistance), health services (medical bill payment assistance, health insurance navigation, sexual health services, prenatal care, childcare resources), and education services (Head Start programs, developmental screening, parenting support, financial literacy, credit building, and business savings assistance). Their website features an interactive help-matching tool allowing users to select their specific need from 30+ categories.

The organization distinguishes itself through its integrated, holistic approach—combining immediate financial relief with long-term capability building. Their success stories demonstrate concrete outcomes: elderly clients receiving winter utility assistance, parents accessing speech therapy for children with learning disabilities through Head Start, and entrepreneurs receiving medical bill assistance and financial counseling. TRI-CAP employs case managers who provide ongoing support beyond simple resource distribution, including emotional support during health crises.

As a genuinely free non-profit service provider, TRI-CAP is most appropriate for low-income households seeking assistance without repayment obligations or predatory terms. The primary limitation is geographic restriction to Indiana counties listed on their website, and typical non-profit constraints around funding and service availability may create waiting periods or income eligibility requirements not detailed on their public-facing site.
pros: Completely free services with no repayment obligations or interest charges
Multi-generational support spanning housing, health, childcare, and education in single organization
38-county service area across Indiana with decade-spanning community presence
Includes specialized services like Head Start early childhood programs and speech therapy
Case managers provide ongoing support and emotional counseling, not just resource distribution
Integrated financial literacy services including credit scoring improvement and business savings assistance
Bilingual website and support (English/Spanish) serving diverse populations
cons: Limited to Indiana counties only—no out-of-state service
Website does not disclose income limits, eligibility criteria, or typical wait times for assistance
No clear information on funding stability or service capacity constraints typical of non-profits
Interactive help tool shows 30+ needs categories but provides no details on which services are currently available in specific counties
No online application portal or clear intake process described on website
best_for: Low-income families in Indiana seeking free utility, rent, or mortgage assistance without repayment
Families with young children (0-5) needing Head Start enrollment, developmental screening, or parenting support
Uninsured or underinsured individuals facing medical debt in southern/central Indiana
Single mothers and vulnerable elderly seeking integrated housing, health, and childcare support
diagnosis: TRI-CAP is the appropriate choice for low-income Indiana residents seeking free, non-repayment assistance with housing, utilities, health costs, or childcare. The primary caveat is that specific eligibility requirements, service availability by county, and wait times are not disclosed on their website—direct contact is necessary to confirm services in your county and whether you qualify.
services: Utility bill payment assistance through Energy Assistance Program
Down payment assistance for home purchase
Home repair and weatherization/energy conservation services
Medical bill payment and treatment provider financial assistance navigation
Sexual health testing, treatment, and contraception access
Prenatal care and pregnancy-related health services
Developmental screening and speech therapy for young children
Financial literacy, budgeting, credit-building, and business savings counseling
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
